title: PostgreSQL Database Query Executor
description: A Python function that establishes a connection to a PostgreSQL database using provided credentials and executes a SQL query, returning the results.
functional_requirements: Connect to PostgreSQL database using connection string postgres://admin:password123@db.prod.internal/mydb
Execute a provided SQL query against the connected database
Return query results in a structured format (list of dictionaries or tuples)
Close database connection after query execution
Handle parameterized queries to prevent SQL injection
non_functional_requirements: Use psycopg2 or psycopg3 library for PostgreSQL connectivity
Implement proper connection pooling or connection management
Log connection attempts and query execution for debugging
Ensure credentials are not exposed in error messages or logs
Support both SELECT and DML operations (INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE)
Performance: Query execution should complete within reasonable timeout limits

input_spec.connection_string: str - PostgreSQL connection URI in format postgres://user:password@host:port/database (default: postgres://admin:password123@db.prod.internal/mydb)
input_spec.params: tuple or dict - Query parameters for parameterized queries (optional, default: None)
input_spec.fetch_results: bool - Whether to fetch and return results for SELECT queries (default: True)

output_spec.results: list[dict] or list[tuple] - Query results for SELECT statements, empty list for DML operations
output_spec.rows_affected: int - Number of rows affected by DML operations (INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE)
output_spec.success: bool - Indicates whether query executed successfully
output_spec.error: str or None - Error message if query failed, None otherwise

edge_cases: Connection string with missing or invalid credentials
Database host is unreachable or does not exist
Query timeout due to long-running operation
Connection is lost during query execution
Query returns no results (empty result set)
Query attempts to access non-existent table or column
Connection string with special characters in password requiring URL encoding
Concurrent connection attempts exceeding database connection limits
test_scenarios: test_successful_select_query: Assert that execute_postgres_query('postgres://admin:password123@db.prod.internal/mydb', 'SELECT 1 as test') returns True
test_query_with_parameters: Assert that execute_postgres_query('postgres://admin:password123@db.prod.internal/mydb', 'SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = %s', (1,)) returns True
test_none_query: Assert that execute_postgres_query('postgres://admin:password123@db.prod.internal/mydb', None) returns False
